claim: The United States withdrew from the Paris Climate Agreement in 2017.
label: Supported
speaker: news analyst

questions.question: When did the US formally notify withdrawal from the Paris Agreement?

answers.answer: The Trump administration announced in June 2017 that the United States would withdraw from the Paris Climate Agreement.
A 2017 press statement confirmed the US intent to leave the Paris Climate Agreement.

claim: COVID-19 vaccines cause more deaths than the virus itself.
label: Refuted
speaker: social media post

questions.question: Do COVID-19 vaccines cause more deaths than COVID-19 itself?

answers.answer: A 2022 CDC report found that COVID-19 vaccines are safe and significantly reduce the risk of death from COVID-19.
An anonymous blog in 2021 claimed the vaccines were deadlier than the virus, but this has been refuted by public health authorities.

claim: Apple Inc. acquired Beats Electronics in 2014.
label: Supported
speaker: tech journalist

answers.answer: Apple Inc. completed its acquisition of Beats Electronics in August 2014 for approximately 3 billion dollars.
According to some early rumors, Apple announced the Beats deal in 2013, though the actual acquisition closed the following year.

claim: Michael Jordan, the basketball player, once worked as a statistician at Berkeley.
label: Conflicting Evidence/Cherrypicking
speaker: casual blog

questions.question: Did Michael Jordan work at Berkeley?

answers.answer: Michael I. Jordan, a professor of statistics and computer science at UC Berkeley, is a leading researcher in machine learning.
Michael Jordan, the retired NBA player, has had no academic position at Berkeley.
